title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 -  5 -  2017: Google Earthing Rome again bitches!

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on December 6, 2017, describing his obsessive Google Earth project to recreate ancient Rome in precise detail — mapping roads, tracing apartment buildings, and distributing 750,000 people across the city's 14 Augustan regions. He also explains why he shelved a parallel castle-mapping project and shares his prediction that he'll finish the Rome model by end of winter.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: ancient Roman demographics and urban layout

stances.position: The only real way to understand what Rome looked like is to build it in Google Earth; he's committed to doing it correctly even if it takes years
Best existing Rome model but it stalled around 2008 and left a gap he intends to fill
Once you get far enough in you pass the point of no return and it's better to push forward than quit
Needs to vlog every day to keep videos short; long gaps lead to marathon catch-up sessions

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't do a V-log since December 1st because I kept staying up until 3, 4, or 5 in the morning and then getting too tired.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to get in the habit of doing a mirror layer, but I don't do it because I got into a new project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was collecting pictures of castles all over Europe and the world and connecting them from my Flickr to Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to start England from scratch because I didn't have any pictures on Flickr for it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I uploaded 420 pictures but only got 1.15 up because some timed out and I skipped them instead of hitting try again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It took all day to upload because I downloaded big versions from Wikipedia.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm now working on a new project called Google Earthing Room.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently mapping roads throughout the entire city of Rome within the Aurelian Wall.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: The Aurelian Wall at its height enclosed around 750,000 to a million people, with about 25% outside the wall.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to finish mapping everything inside the Aurelian Wall first, then do everything outside, and finally map the people.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: There were 42,000 to 46,000 apartment buildings in Rome, which I'm approximating to 45,000.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: There were 1,790 domus (houses) in Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I divided Rome into 14 regions based on the Augustan regions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm calculating population density by area and tracing buildings to recreate ancient Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm using extra vision to see how buildings looked 2,000 years ago and tracing them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to color-code buildings by height, with different colors for each story.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: A single-person apartment room in Rome was about 3.5 meters by 4 meters.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm mathematically calculating where each of the 750,000 people would have lived in Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got burned out tracing buildings because it was too tedious, so I switched to mapping roads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I barely worked today because I got up late and had to shampoo my mom's house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I earned $15 for shampooing my mom's house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might finish mapping the roads today and then start tracing buildings again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimate I could finish mapping Rome by the end of winter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once I finish Rome, I can make a lot of high-quality YouTube videos about it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The only way to truly understand what Rome looked like is by building it in Google Earth.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: The seven hills of Rome aren't pronounced today, but recreating them in Google Earth would show their historical significance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to Aristotle's 'Politics' and a list of medieval technologies.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: You can download my Google Earth files from my website to see my progress on mapping Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tend to talk for a long time in my videos, so I need to do shorter daily videos.
